channel.title: The Git Reporter
channel.description: A code-native newspaper of meaningful software change.

item.title: Entire Gives Agent Work a Separate Git Address
item.description: The CLI stores prompts, tool calls, touched files, and now images on a checkpoint branch—making an AI coding session resumable, and potentially shareable by accident.

item.title: API Discovery: Check a Barcode Against France's Recall List
item.description: France's official RappelConso data can turn a scanned GTIN into a product-recall match—if the agent checks the lot, date, and government notice instead of treating a name hit as proof.

item.title: Builder Work: OpenClaw Lets Old Agent Runs Die
item.description: An AI-assisted OpenClaw fix grew into a generation-scoped restart protocol after maintainers tested what happens when an old and live subagent share a session.

item.title: Gemini CLI Explains the Missing Privacy Switch
item.description: The /privacy screen now says when a consumer data-control does not apply to an account, instead of making Workspace and enterprise users decode a raw OAuth or tier error.

item.title: OpenWork Makes MCP Sessions Durable
item.description: OpenWork's July 9 commits changed cloud auth and MCP client state so agent connections can survive refresh, offline access, and cross-origin client paths.

item.title: API Discovery: Impact CO2 Turns Carbon Data Into Agent Estimates
item.description: API Discovery: ADEME's Impact CO2 API gives builders a public way to turn transport, heating, food, and everyday-object emissions into quick agent-readable estimates.

item.title: Builder Work: ContextMatrix Makes Agent Boards Git-Auditable
item.description: ContextMatrix is today's Builder Work Highlight because it shows an AI-assisted coordination pattern: keep tasks in git, let agents use MCP, and make execution backends prove themselves through signed handoffs.

item.title: Karpathy's Educational Open-Source Factory
item.description: From micrograd to nanochat, Andrej Karpathy's public code curriculum advances by exposing one more layer, preserving a trusted answer key, and deleting whatever makes the next edition harder to read.

item.title: Pi Turns Cache Misses Into Budget Signals
item.description: Pi's Daily Story is a project-under-pressure story: the coding agent now makes prompt-cache misses, long-input pricing, and thinking levels visible where builders choose how much context to spend.

item.title: Agent Runtimes Move Trust To The Exit Gate
item.description: OpenClaw, Codex, and Hermes all changed runtime trust surfaces this week, making the last moment before a tool call, secret handoff, or approval the place operators need to inspect.

item.title: API Discovery: Ma Cantine Turns Lunch Rules Into Agent Checklists
item.description: API Discovery: DINUM's Ma Cantine API exposes canteens, diagnostics, and declaration data that an agent can turn into a compliance-prep checklist for cafeteria software teams.

item.title: Builder Work: Ralphy Gives Coding Agents A Video Runtime
item.description: Ralphy is today's Builder Work Highlight because Alec's repo turns a coding agent into the operator of a reproducible video pipeline, not just a prompt wrapper around media APIs.

item.title: LangChain Stops Retries From Eating Interrupts
item.description: LangChain's retry middleware repair is a people/context story because the project's public human-in-the-loop work makes one small bug fix read as a guardrail for users trying to stop an agent.

item.title: Gemini CLI Turns Issue Triage Into A Caretaker Worker
item.description: Gemini CLI added a caretaker triage worker and GitHub egress handler, moving issue sorting toward an agent-run workflow with locks, retries, labels, comments, and human handoff states.

item.title: API Discovery: RNB Turns Buildings Into Agent-Readable IDs
item.description: Compact source brief, not the lead: the RNB building API is narrow but useful because it gives agents a public building identifier before they draft local permit, retrofit, insurance, or asset records.

item.title: Builder Work: Zerodecimal Makes Vibe Coding Prove Arithmetic
item.description: Alexandros Kyriakakis publicly said zerodecimal was built with Claude Code, Ultracode, and Fable 5; the repo is worth studying because the generated arithmetic is surrounded by proof scaffolding.

item.title: Harness Engineering Is Becoming The Field's Self-Improvement Layer
item.description: Lilian Weng's July 4 map of self-improving harnesses gives builders a constructive middle path: improve the runtime around the model, but keep evidence, permissions, and evaluation legible enough that the loop can be trusted.

item.title: Self-Evolving Agents Need Receipts For The Loop
item.description: Shilong Liu's taxonomy widens the self-improvement story beyond harnesses: agents may optimize artifacts, modify the harness, or learn in the model, but each loop needs evidence about what changed and what stayed outside the optimizer.

item.title: Shepherd Makes Safety Evidence A Release Story
item.description: Shepherd's 0.3.0 release commit is a Daily Story because Derek Chong did not only ship features; the public release note makes executed safety evidence part of the project contract.

item.title: OpenClaw Makes Agent Audit Trails A Runtime Contract
item.description: OpenClaw added a metadata-only audit ledger on July 6, giving operators a way to inspect agent run events, native-search outcomes, tool actions, statuses, and errors without opening full transcripts.

item.title: API Discovery: data.gouv.fr Turns Dataset Search Into Agent Intake
item.description: The data.gouv.fr catalogue API gives agents a public way to search French datasets, inspect metadata, and find attached resources before they act on a claim about public data.

item.title: Builder Work: Live Log Viewer Maps Parallel Agents
item.description: Live Log Viewer reads local Claude Code and Codex transcripts, reconstructs session parentage, and turns parallel agent work into a live map without adding a cloud service or database.

item.title: Codex Pins Network Denials To The Exact Exec
item.description: Codex's July 6 Linux network-attribution fix turns a vague shared-proxy denial into a traceable event tied to the exact exec call and command that opened the connection.

item.title: Hermes Makes Yolo Obey User Deny Rules
item.description: Hermes added `approvals.deny` on July 5, giving users glob rules that block terminal commands even when the agent is running in yolo or approvals-off mode.

item.title: API Discovery: Recherche d'entreprises Turns Company Search Into Agent Triage
item.description: API Recherche d'entreprises exposes public French company search through text, SIREN/SIRET, geography, NAF, and employee-size filters.

item.title: Builder Work: Mycelium Makes Agents Prove The Product Before Coding
item.description: Haabe's Mycelium is an open-source Claude Code plugin that was publicly described as built using itself and repo-inspected as a decision framework with receipts, git-stored state, and agent skills.

item.title: OpenWork Turns OpenCode Into A Shareable Agent Host
item.description: OpenWork's source trail shows a desktop app, server, orchestrator, router, file sessions, approval flow, export safety, and single-org deployment policy around OpenCode rather than a thin UI wrapper.

item.title: Pi Turns Issue Analysis Into A Shareable Session Trail
item.description: Pi's July 6 commits changed issue analysis from a CI artifact into a staff-triggered, gist-backed session that can be summarized in public and imported back into a local checkout.

item.title: OpenCode Turns OpenAPI Into Agent Tool Contracts
item.description: OpenCode merged a CodeMode OpenAPI adapter on July 4, giving hosts a way to turn API specs into model-visible tools while keeping credentials and unsupported operations outside the agent's reach.

item.title: API Discovery: API Association Turns Nonprofit Identity Into Agent Checks
item.description: API Associations exposes French nonprofit identity data from the RNA and Sirene sources, giving agents a public way to verify an association before drafting eligibility, grant, or onboarding work.
